content:
# Critical Flow

**Summary**: A flow regime where the downstream pressure has no effect on the upstream pressure due to high velocity.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992]] focused on critical flow patterns for choke design purposes.

- [[1992-al-towailib-2|Al-Towailib, 1992]] focused on data under critical flow patterns, defined as downstream tubing pressure less than 55% of upstream tubing pressure.

## Scientific Context

- [[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992]] Identifying critical flow is essential for accurate choke sizing as it defines the boundary of reliable calculation.

- [[1992-al-towailib-2|Al-Towailib, 1992]] focused on data under critical flow patterns, defined as downstream tubing pressure less than 55% of upstream tubing pressure.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- data-analysis

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The correlation applies for critical flow conditions which makes it useful for choke design purposes. ([[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992, p. 20]])


- 335 tests were dropped because they did not meet the critical flow criteria (downstream pressure < 55% of upstream). ([[1992-al-towailib-2|Al-Towailib, 1992, p. 61]])
